description: Timely Topics SOME THOUGHTS ON THE RUS SIAN REVOLUTION The attempt to explain the Russian revolution which the events of the last few days have shown to he still in a preliminary stage of development hy submit ting it to the test of rational logic is bound to prove futile We can set a definite period for its dura tion as little as we can discover its ultiinale effect upon Russia and the world beyond its borders by ap plying to it the historical rule of three The story of revolutions the world over and in all ages is that pure reason has very little to do with them Although not in frequently conceived in rational logic they arc never brought into being and developed without the concurrence of those affective and mystic elements which alone can arouse men to action The researches of Gustave Lc Bon have proved conclusively that a people is as incapable of con ceiving a revolution as the leaders of a revolution without the sup port of the people are of carrying it out The machinery of a perfect revolution is made up of ideas leaders army and mob The phi losophers whose ideas paved
